title: Fanout Pub/Sub and Subscriber Responsibilities · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs fanout pub/sub and subscriber responsibilities from producer, broker, queue, delivery, Go runtime, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one protocol or concurrency misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a Go subscriber responsibility and queue lifecycle map after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Give each independent subscriber responsibility its own queue so every responsibility can receive and acknowledge its copy; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Use fanout routing when every binding should match and record the resulting copy count and storage responsibility; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Design exclusive server-named subscriptions for session-scoped state and account for disconnect, binding, declaration, and cleanup races; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility · changed case · Review Subscriber Queue Per Re…
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go messaging team handles case 8512 during table-driven topology review for a tenant migration. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove one queue per subscriber responsibility and explicit late-subscriber behavior; prove cleanup under a single deadline and cancellation cause; then cancel the parent context while a result is still ambiguous. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Give each independent subscriber responsibility its own queue so every responsibility can receive and acknowledge its copy; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Which action demonstrates Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility without dropping earlier contracts?

Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: State whether late subscribers miss prior events, receive retained queue backlog, or require a stream or database replay path; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Late Subscriber Semantics, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A new fanout binding automatically receives messages published before it existed. In Go, multiple goroutines consuming one queue still implement competing consumers, not broadcast.
